summary:
Enlarged blind spots are classically associated with papilledema caused by increased intracranial pressure. This condition results from swelling of the optic disk and leads directly to an expansion of the physiological blind spot within the visual fields.  

Final Answer: \boxed{D}

The summary should not include any additional commentary or explanation beyond what was provided in Agent1’s original response.

Summary:  
Enlarged blind spots are a hallmark sign of papilledema resulting from raised intracranial pressure. This ocular manifestation arises due tooptic disc swelling and reflects underlying increases in cerebrospinal fluid pressure.
